chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of the Rheonics SRD Varinline inline density and viscosity meter, detailing its features, applications, and installation advantages, particularly when used with GEA Varinline connections.
product_names: SRD Inline density and viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
SRD Varinline: Inline density and viscosity meter with Varinline flange
Modified on: Fri, 25 Oct, 2024 at 7:33 PM

What products are involved in this article?
SRD Inline density and viscosity meter. (https://es.rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To give an overview of the SRD inline density and viscosity meter with GEA Varinline connection, its uses, and installation options. (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/s)

1. Overview
(https://es.rheonics.com/productos/viscos%C3%ADmetro-en-l%C3%ADn%C3%ADa-srd/)
The SRD is a Rheonics sensor for inline density and viscosity measurements. Read more on SRD here (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).

The Figure 1 shows a flush variant SRD inline density and viscosity meter with a Varinline Process Connection. This design configuration can be used to make the SRD sensor probe compatible with different kinds of GEA Varinline housings (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp), which are mostly used for the installation of hygienic instruments in hygienic or sanitary applications.

The SRD probe with a Varinline connection can be ordered with an EHEDG or 3-A certification. Learn more about these certifications here (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412134-ehedg-and-3-a).

The advantages of using the SRD sensor with a Varinline process connection in GEA housings are that it:
* Removes dead zones around the sensing element
* Ensures easy cleaning and sterilization through standard CIP (Clean in place) procedure.
* Allows perpendicular installation in small lines such as DN40 or DN50*.

*Installation in DN40 or DN50 GEA Housings require the use of a machined plug from Rheonics. This machined plug has a cut that maintains the SRD sensing area unobstructed. This machined plug is hygienic but not certified with EHEDG or 3-A. See more in section 2 of this article.
key_facts: The SRD is a Rheonics sensor designed for inline density and viscosity measurements.
The SRD Varinline variant features a Varinline Process Connection for compatibility with GEA Varinline housings.
This design is primarily used for installing hygienic instruments in hygienic or sanitary applications.
The SRD probe with a Varinline connection can be ordered with EHEDG or 3-A certification.

Advantages of using the SRD sensor with a Varinline process connection in GEA housings include removing dead zones, ensuring easy CIP (Clean in Place), and allowing perpendicular installation in small lines (DN40 or DN50).
warnings_notes: Installation in DN40 or DN50 GEA Housings requires a machined plug from Rheonics.
The Rheonics machined plug maintains the SRD sensing area unobstructed.
The Rheonics machined plug is hygienic but not certified with EHEDG or 3-A.
Further details on the machined plug are available in section 2 of this article.

A Varinline connection is composed of a Plug, an O-ring seal, and a Clamp. In case the SRD Varinline sensor is used, this replaces the plug.
The SRD Varinline sensor probe can be installed in different GEA housings or adapters like the ones listed in the next table.

Table 2: Suitable GEA housings or adapters for the SRD Varinline sensor.

GEA Variant	Description	Referral Image
(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)
Varinline Housings	Straight housing with constant cross-section. Allows the SRD Varinline to be installed perpendicular in-line in small pipes (DN40 minimum). GEA Varinline housing pipes can follow different norms such as ISO 11850, DIN11866, ISO1127 AME-BPE, and IPS.	GEA Reference (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)
Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA)	Straight housing with constant cross-section and sight glass. Allows the SRD Varinline to be installed in a Varinline housing (DN50 minimum) with a glass at the other side to see the fluid and check the probe without dismantling.	GEA Reference (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-sight-glass-txia/)
Varinline Tank connection flange (Type P)	Adapter for tanks. Allows a flush installation of the SRD Varinline in tanks.	GEA Reference (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments/)
key_facts: A Varinline connection is composed of a Plug, an O-ring seal, and a Clamp.
The SRD Varinline sensor replaces the plug when used in a Varinline connection.
The SRD Varinline sensor probe can be installed in various GEA housings or adapters.
Varinline Housings provide a straight, constant cross-section for perpendicular in-line installation of the SRD Varinline in small pipes.
Varinline Sight Glass (Type TXIA) housings feature a straight cross-section and a sight glass, allowing fluid observation and probe inspection without dismantling.
Varinline Tank connection flanges (Type P) are adapters for tanks, facilitating flush installation of the SRD Varinline.
GEA Varinline housing pipes comply with multiple international norms, including ISO 11850, DIN11866, ISO1127 AME-BPE, and IPS.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides detailed installation instructions and component lists for Rheonics SRD inline viscometers and density sensors in various Varinline and GEA housings, including a flush installation drawing with dimensions.

Inline density and viscosity meter - SRD with Varinline N Machined Process connection

https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp

2. Installation examples

2.1 GEA Housings (Type T)

2.1.1 DIN11850 DN40 - Inline flow cell DN40
This solution allows a perpendicular installation of the SRD sensor probe in a straight DN40 or 1.5” line. This installation requires a Varinline N machined plug provided by Rheonics (PLG-VNM) as the probe connection and the blind plug, as shown in Figure 2.

The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with the Rheonics SRD. If smaller housings are required contact Rheonics Support Team.

The installation in Figure 2, requires the following components. The whole assembly can be delivered by Rheonics but the client may already have the GEA accessories or can order them separately.

*   SRD-X4 Varinline N Machined from Rheonics.
*   Varinline Housing DN40
*   Varinline Machined N Plug (PLG-VNM) from Rheonics
*   Varinline DN40 Clamps.
*   O-ring Seal.

Table 3: SRD-X4 in Varinline Housing DN40

https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/SRD-X4-VNM_Housing-DN40.pdf
Units: mm [inch]

2.1.2 DIN11850 DN50 or DN65
The SRD can be installed in the Varinline DN50 or DN65 Housing. For the DN50 or DN65 line, the probe still requires the Machined N Plug as a process connection, but the blind plug on the housing is not required to be machined, as shown in Figure 5.
key_facts: Varinline Housing Connection Flange (Type U and U-S) allows installations of the SRD Varinline in Tee or elbows.
The DIN11850 DN40 solution allows a perpendicular installation of the SRD sensor probe in a straight DN40 or 1.5” line.
This installation requires a Varinline N machined plug provided by Rheonics (PLG-VNM) as the probe connection and the blind plug.
The Varinline DN40 is currently the smallest GEA Housing compatible with the Rheonics SRD.
The installation for DN40 requires SRD-X4 Varinline N Machined from Rheonics, Varinline Housing DN40, Varinline Machined N Plug (PLG-VNM) from Rheonics, Varinline DN40 Clamps, and an O-ring Seal.
The whole assembly for the DIN11850 DN40 installation can be delivered by Rheonics, or the client may already have the GEA accessories or order them separately.
Varinline Housing DN40 can also refer to GEA Housings that comply with ISO 1127 - 33.7 or ASME - BPE - OD 1.5” standards.
The SRD can be installed in Varinline DN50 or DN65 Housing.
For the DN50 or DN65 line, the probe still requires the Machined N Plug as a process connection, but the blind plug on the housing is not required to be machined.
warnings_notes: If smaller housings are required contact Rheonics Support Team.

2.1.3 DIN11850 DN80 or bigger .
If a housing DIN11850 DN80 is used, the probe doesn't require the Machined N Plug anymore (nor the housing's blind plug ), it can be used with the common Varinline N plug as a process connection, see an example in Figure 6.
When a bigger housing is used (e.g., DN100) the SRD short probe can be used. The SRD short (SRD-X2) would have an advantage over the flush variant (SRD-X4) because the sensing element will be closer to the middle of the pipe.
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410685-srd-x2-custom-flange)
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411849-srd-x4-flush-sensor-for-hygienic-and-sanitary-applications)

Varinline Housing DN80* | GEA | TDN80 | (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp)

* ISO 1127 - 60.3
* ASME - BPE - OD 3"
* IPS - 3"
key_facts: Rheonics offers SRD-X4 inline density and viscosity meters with specific Varinline N Machined Process connections.
For DIN11850 DN80 housings, the SRD probe does not require a Machined N Plug or the housing's blind plug, using a common Varinline N plug instead.
The SRD-X2 short probe is advantageous over the SRD-X4 flush variant for larger housings (e.g., DN100) because its sensing element is positioned closer to the middle of the pipe.
Varinline Housings DN50 and DN80 can also be sourced from GEA, with specified ISO, ASME, and IPS compatibility standards.
warnings_notes: Varinline Housing DN50 can also use GEA Housings with ISO 1127 - 42.4, ASME - BPE - OD 2", and IPS - 2" standards.
If a housing DIN11850 DN80 is used, the probe doesn't require the Machined N Plug anymore (nor the housing's blind plug), it can be used with the common Varinline N plug as a process connection.

When a bigger housing is used (e.g., DN100) the SRD short probe can be used. The SRD short (SRD-X2) would have an advantage over the flush variant (SRD-X4) because the sensing element will be closer to the middle of the pipe.
Varinline Housing DN80 can also use GEA Housings with ISO 1127 - 60.3, ASME - BPE - OD 3", and IPS - 3" standards.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides technical drawings, installation examples, and product information for Rheonics SRD inline viscometer and density sensors integrated with Varinline housings and sight glasses, including options for concentric reducers.
chunks.extracted_text:
2.1.4 Varinline Sight glass
The sight glass allows you to see the inside of the pipe without disassembling the sensor. This is useful when visual monitoring is desired to check if the SRD probe is clean or if it has deposits/fouling.

SRD - Varinline Hygienic Installation Example
Description | Supplier | Order code | Drawing
Inline density and viscosity meter - SRD with Varinline N Machined Process connection | Rheonics | SRD-X4-VNM |
Varinline Housing DN50 | GEA (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) | TDN50 | SRD-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50_SightGlass (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/SRD-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50_SightGlass.pdf)
Varinline Sight glass | GEA (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) | TXIAN-50 |

Figure 6. Flush installation drawing of SRD in DN80 Varinline Housing
(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/SRD-X4-VN_Housing-DN80.pdf)

Figure 7. Flush installation drawing of SRD in DN50 Varinline Housing with sight glass
(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/SRD-X4-VNM_Housing-DN50_SightGlass.pdf)

2.1.5 Varinline Sight glass - concentric reducers
Following the previous point, we can add concentric reducers to the housing ports (e.g. DN50-DN38 reducers), as in Figure 7, to mount the sensor in smaller lines. You can weld the concentric reducers to the line or use Tri-clamp ferrules for easy disassembly.
key_facts: The Varinline Sight glass allows visual monitoring inside the pipe without disassembling the sensor.
Visual monitoring helps check if the SRD probe is clean or if it has deposits/fouling.
Concentric reducers can be added to housing ports (e.g., DN50-DN38 reducers) to mount the sensor in smaller lines.
Concentric reducers can be welded to the line or use Tri-clamp ferrules for easy disassembly.
Rheonics supplies the SRD-X4-VNM inline density and viscosity meter with a Varinline N Machined Process connection.
GEA is a supplier for Varinline Housing DN50 (Order code TDN50) and Varinline Sight glass (Order code TXIAN-50).

2.2 GEA Tank connection flange (Type P)
The SRD can be installed in GEA's Varinline tank connection flange, which is welded into vessels with a wall thickness up to 20 mm to avoid dead zones in a hygienic installation.

SRD - Varinline Hygienic Installation Example

Varinline tank connection flange | GEA (https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp) | TPN |

SRD Varinline Tank Connection flange
Part number | Part description
1 | SRD-X4-Varinline N
2 | Welded tank varinline flange
3 | O-ring N
4 | Grooved flange x2
5 | Bolt x4

Another installation option for the tank connection flange is using an SRD Long Insertion probe (SRD-X5) (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411193-srd-x5-long-insertion-probe), this is very useful when you need a longer immersion of the sensing element in the tank.
key_facts: The SRD inline density and viscosity meter can be installed using GEA's Varinline tank connection flange.
The Varinline tank connection flange is designed to be welded into vessels with a wall thickness up to 20 mm.
This installation method helps prevent dead zones, crucial for hygienic applications.
An SRD Long Insertion probe (SRD-X5) provides an alternative installation option for scenarios requiring deeper immersion of the sensing element in the tank.
warnings_notes: The Varinline tank connection flange is welded into vessels to avoid dead zones in a hygienic installation.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an overview of VARINLINE technology, detailing its features, benefits, applications, and compatibility with Rheonics viscometer and density sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
3. What is Varinline?

VARINLINE is a trademark of GEA for a range of in-line control and measurement technology for hygienic process connections. It allows the installation of control and measuring instruments in piping systems without any dead spaces, which is important in applications where hygiene is critical, such as the food and beverage industry.
VARINLINE housings are available for all pipe sizes and can accommodate a variety of instruments, including pressure gauges, temperature sensors, and flow meters. The housings are self-draining, which helps to prevent the build-up of contaminants. They are also CIP/SIP-able, which means that they can be cleaned and sterilized without the need to dismantle them.
VARINLINE is a popular choice for a variety of hygienic process applications, including:
* Food and beverage processing
* Pharmaceutical manufacturing
* Biotechnology
* Cosmetics production
* Water treatment
It is a reliable and cost-effective solution for the monitoring and control of process parameters in hygienic environments.
Here are some of the benefits of using VARINLINE:
* Hygienic design: VARINLINE housings are designed to prevent the build-up of contaminants, making them ideal for use in hygienic environments.
* CIP/SIP-able: VARINLINE housings can be cleaned and sterilized without the need to dismantle them, which helps to ensure the safety and quality of the product.
* Wide range of applications: VARINLINE is a versatile solution that can be used in a variety of applications, including food and beverage processing, pharmaceutical manufacturing, and biotechnology.
* Reliable and durable: VARINLINE housings are made from high-quality materials and are designed to withstand the harsh conditions found in industrial environments.
If you are looking for a reliable and cost-effective solution for the monitoring and control of process parameters in hygienic environments, VARINLINE is a good option to consider.
Rheonics provides EHEDG certified inline process viscometer SRV and inline process density meter SRD with Varinline process connection to work with GEA Varinline adapters.
The sensors are also available with EX certifications where needed. Standard sensors are available in Stainless steel 316L and for corrosive environments in Hastelloy C22.

Resources

(https://www.gea.com/en/products/pumps-valves-flow-components/valves-components-hygienic-liquid-processing/inline-technology/varinline-pocket-free-instruments.jsp).
[2] Rheonics SRD sensor (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/). (https://www.gea.com/en/binaries/gea-varinline-varicomp-hygienic-process-connections-and-expansion-compensators_tcm11-82365.pdf).
[3] Rheonics EHEDG certification. (https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/Rheonics_SRD-Density-and-Viscosity-Meter_EHEDG-Certificate.pdf)
[4] Learn more about EHEDG. (https://rheonics.com/rheonics-ehedg-certified-inline-viscosity-and-density-sensors-for-food-and-pharmaceutical-applications/)
[5] Inline process viscometer SRV with Varinline flange - options and installation (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000412012-inline-process-viscometer-srv-with-varinline-flange-options-and-installation)

Disclaimer

Pictures, photos, and images used are for illustration purposes and do not constitute any warranty or assertions on the suitability of use and should not be construed as an explicit or implicit recommendation or endorsement. All illustrations are given proper credit from our source of access and by their use here we do not specify or establish any copyright which belongs and stays unaffected with the existing copyright holder of that material.
key_facts: VARINLINE is a trademark of GEA, offering in-line control and measurement technology for hygienic process connections.
VARINLINE housings eliminate dead spaces, are self-draining, and are CIP/SIP-able for easy cleaning and sterilization.
VARINLINE is suitable for hygienic process applications, including food and beverage processing, pharmaceutical manufacturing, biotechnology, cosmetics production, and water treatment.
Rheonics provides EHEDG certified inline process viscometer SRV and inline process density meter SRD designed to work with GEA Varinline adapters.
Rheonics sensors are available with EX certifications for use in hazardous environments.
Standard Rheonics sensors are constructed from Stainless steel 316L, with Hastelloy C22 used for corrosive environments.
